narratives.name: AI Infrastructure Buildout

Technology.thesis: The AI capex cycle continues to be the dominant driver, fueling demand across semiconductors, equipment, and software. Hyperscaler investments and enterprise AI adoption are expanding, leading to strong performance in key segments.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
China export controls expand to HBM or advanced packaging — would disrupt supply chains
Global chip inventory levels rise significantly, indicating oversupply

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: Consumer spending remains resilient in some areas like apparel and online retail, but broader concerns about inflation and economic uncertainty are creating headwinds for discretionary categories and residential construction.
watchFor: Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — forces a hawkish Fed repricing
Retail sales data shows significant contraction
Consumer confidence index drops sharply

Communication Services.thesis: The sector is seeing a push towards AI integration and content expansion, with some companies benefiting from new initiatives, while others face competitive pressures and valuation concerns.
watchFor: Major tech companies pull back on AI investments
New streaming service subscriber growth stalls across the board
Regulatory actions target large internet platforms

Industrials.thesis: Defense spending and space-related initiatives are driving strong backlogs and positive sentiment for aerospace and defense companies, while integrated logistics also sees positive analyst attention.
watchFor: Significant cuts to defense budgets globally
Major geopolitical de-escalation reduces demand for defense products
Global trade slowdown impacts freight volumes

Healthcare.thesis: The GLP-1 obesity drug wave continues to drive significant growth and positive analyst sentiment for pharmaceutical companies, while healthcare plans also see positive outlooks.
watchFor: New GLP-1 competitors emerge with superior efficacy or fewer side effects
Regulatory hurdles or safety concerns for existing GLP-1 drugs
PBMs exert significant pricing pressure on pharmaceutical companies

Financials.thesis: The financial sector is showing strength with positive analyst sentiment and strong Q1 earnings for major banks, while credit services and asset management also see favorable outlooks.
watchFor: Unexpected interest rate cuts by the Fed, compressing NIMs
Significant increase in loan defaults or credit losses
New regulatory burdens on banks or financial services

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The consumer defensive sector shows resilience in grocery traffic for discount stores, but broader economic concerns and mixed analyst sentiment create a neutral outlook.
watchFor: Inflation re-accelerates, squeezing consumer discretionary budgets
Unemployment rate rises, impacting consumer spending power
Significant shift in consumer preferences away from discount retailers

Utilities.thesis: The utilities sector is benefiting from strong analyst sentiment and strategic agreements, particularly in independent power production and renewable energy, driven by increasing energy demand and sustainability initiatives.
watchFor: Unexpected regulatory changes impacting energy prices or subsidies
Significant increase in interest rates, raising financing costs
Slowdown in renewable energy project development

Basic Materials.thesis: The industrial metals and mining sector is facing mixed signals, with some companies seeing volatility despite new partnerships, indicating a cautious outlook.
watchFor: Global industrial production slows significantly
Commodity prices experience a sharp downturn
Increased geopolitical tensions disrupt supply chains

Precious Metals.thesis: Gold is gaining focus amidst geopolitical developments and climbing bullion prices, positioning it as a potential safe-haven asset, while silver also shows some positive momentum.
watchFor: Stronger-than-expected economic growth reduces safe-haven demand
Significant strengthening of the US dollar
De-escalation of geopolitical tensions
